title: Beacon Hill: Phasing Periodic Transmissions on a Shared Channel
strategies: duty-cycle greedy: sort jobs by d_i/p_i descending, place each at the offset of least exact marginal waste -- places the high-duty flexible family first, scattering it over expensive residue classes as singles and forcing late rigid jobs' unavoidable overlap onto heavy instants

CRT residue tiling: detect the cheap residue class c* mod 4 from the posted weights; route every d=1, 4|p job wholesale into c* (their mutual excess is priced at the cheap rate), place rigid jobs first on the expensive classes while still empty, then exact-marginal local search
headroom above strong: joint optimization of rigid-family staggering (which rigid pairs may align at gcd beats), better use of leftover cheap-class capacity, smarter local search / ILP-ish moves
